kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: More members of the River Ward are in the Joiners' Guild than members of the Market Ward.
Either Kingsford goes with Beatrix or someone in the Joiners' Guild goes with Beatrix (but not both).
Riverside goes with at least two of Beatrix, Carpenter, and 13 coins.
Highrow goes with exactly one of Wulfric, 10 coins, or 11 coins.
Between Rowan and Blacksmith, one goes with 8 coins and the other with 10 coins.
Rowan's levy is higher than both the Carpenter and the artisan in Kingsford.
Edmund goes with exactly two of Cooper, Kingsford, and 12 coins.
The Tanner's levy is lower than both Godfrey and the artisan in Oldwall.
Between Aldric and Kingsford, one goes with Carpenter and the other with 8 coins.
Exactly two of these are true: Carpenter goes with 9 coins; Cooper goes with 10 coins; Potter goes with Riverside.
Aldric, Blacksmith, and Oldwall belong to different artisans.
Either Cooper goes with Millpond or someone in the Market Ward goes with Cooper (but not both).
Eastcheap goes with exactly one of Aldric, Beatrix, or Tanner.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: More members of the South Watershed are in the Pike Family than members of the West Watershed.
Either Grayreach goes with Cody or someone in the Pike Family goes with Cody (but not both).
Willowmere goes with at least two of Cody, Carp, and 20 lb.
Foxglen goes with exactly one of Wade, 14 lb, or 16 lb.
Between Gus and Bass, one goes with 10 lb and the other with 14 lb.
Gus' weight is higher than both the angler who landed the Carp and the angler at Grayreach.
Dale goes with exactly two of Crappie, Grayreach, and 18 lb.
The angler who landed the Pike's weight is lower than both Esther and the angler at Stillwater.
Between Boone and Grayreach, one goes with Carp and the other with 10 lb.
Exactly two of these are true: Carp goes with 12 lb; Crappie goes with 14 lb; Perch goes with Willowmere.
Boone, Bass, and Stillwater belong to different catches.
Either Crappie goes with Mossbank or someone in the West Watershed goes with Crappie (but not both).
Cedarmere goes with exactly one of Boone, Cody, or Pike.
